mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 in rolling 3-year windows during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent indicates severe labor market slack and collapsing consumer spending, which triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or earnings revisions remain flat.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases but S&P 500 20-day returns remain negative or flat in > 70% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.3 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to halt easing, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.3% is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases without subsequent rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting wage growth and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Divergent PAYEMS and UNRATE trends fail to predict 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or labor cost inflation triggers recession fears.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue generation, which directly translates to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 65% of observations or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.5 percent indicates severe manufacturing contraction and supply chain disruption, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO MoM < -0.5% is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances without macro data revisions.
